211	func NewWorkerManager(projectRoot string) *WorkerManager {
212		m := &WorkerManager{
213			projectRoot:      projectRoot,
214			rootDir:          filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx", "workers"),
215			workers:          map[string]*workerHandle{},
216			LandCoordinators: newCoordinatorRegistry(),
217			watchdogStop:     make(chan struct{}),
218		}
219		m.applyServerWatchdogConfig(projectRoot)
220		return m
221	}

223	// applyServerWatchdogConfig reads .ddx/config.yaml at projectRoot and applies
224	// any server.watchdog_deadline / server.stall_deadline overrides. Invalid or
225	// missing values are silently ignored — defaults are filled in by the
226	// watchdog loop at runtime.
227	func (m *WorkerManager) applyServerWatchdogConfig(projectRoot string) {
228		cfg, err := config.LoadWithWorkingDir(projectRoot)
229		if err != nil || cfg == nil || cfg.Server == nil {
230			return
231		}
232		if d, err := time.ParseDuration(cfg.Server.WatchdogDeadline); err == nil && d > 0 {
233			m.WatchdogDeadline = d
234		}
235		if d, err := time.ParseDuration(cfg.Server.StallDeadline); err == nil && d > 0 {
236			m.StallDeadline = d
237		}
238	}

240	// watchdogDeadlines returns the effective deadlines, applying defaults for
241	// any zero-valued fields.
242	func (m *WorkerManager) watchdogDeadlines() (watchdog, stall, check, grace time.Duration) {
243		watchdog = m.WatchdogDeadline
244		if watchdog <= 0 {
245			watchdog = defaultWatchdogDeadline
246		}
247		stall = m.StallDeadline
248		if stall <= 0 {
249			stall = defaultStallDeadline
250		}
251		check = m.WatchdogCheckInterval
252		if check <= 0 {
253			check = defaultWatchdogCheckInterval
254		}
255		grace = m.WatchdogKillGrace
256		if grace <= 0 {
257			grace = defaultWatchdogKillGrace
258		}
259		return
260	}

262	func (m *WorkerManager) StartExecuteLoop(spec ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec) (WorkerRecord, error) {
263		// Resolve the effective project root: spec override takes priority over the
264		// manager's default so callers can target any registered project.
265		effectiveRoot := spec.ProjectRoot
266		if effectiveRoot == "" {
267			effectiveRoot = m.projectRoot
268		}
269	
270		// Pre-flight: validate harness availability and model compatibility
271		// before creating the worker record or claiming any beads.
272		if err := agent.ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService(context.Background(), effectiveRoot, spec.Harness, spec.Model, spec.Provider, spec.ModelRef); err != nil {
273			return WorkerRecord{}, fmt.Errorf("execute-loop: %w", err)
274		}
275	
276		if err := os.MkdirAll(m.rootDir, 0o755); err != nil {
277			return WorkerRecord{}, err
278		}
279	
280		id := "worker-" + time.Now().UTC().Format("20060102T150405") + "-" + randomSuffix(4)
281		dir := filepath.Join(m.rootDir, id)
282		if err := os.MkdirAll(dir, 0o755); err != nil {
283			return WorkerRecord{}, err
284		}
285	
286		// Write spec
287		specData, _ := json.MarshalIndent(spec, "", "  ")
288		_ = os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(dir, "spec.json"), append(specData, '\n'), 0o644)
289	
290		// Open log file
291		logPath := filepath.Join(dir, "worker.log")
292		logFile, err := os.OpenFile(logPath, os.O_CREATE|os.O_WRONLY|os.O_TRUNC, 0o644)
293		if err != nil {
294			return WorkerRecord{}, err
295		}
296	
297		// Open structured event sink — loop milestones (bead.claimed, bead.result,
298		// loop.start/end) land here as JSONL so log aggregators and future server
299		// endpoints can parse them independently of the human-readable worker.log.
300		eventsPath := filepath.Join(dir, "worker-events.jsonl")
301		eventsFile, eventsErr := os.OpenFile(eventsPath, os.O_CREATE|os.O_WRONLY|os.O_TRUNC, 0o644)
302		if eventsErr != nil {
303			eventsFile = nil // non-fatal; structured events silently disabled
304		}
305	
306		record := WorkerRecord{
307			ID:           id,
308			Kind:         "execute-loop",
309			State:        "running",
310			Status:       "running",
311			ProjectRoot:  effectiveRoot,
312			Harness:      spec.Harness,
313			Model:        spec.Model,
314			Profile:      agent.NormalizeRoutingProfile(spec.Profile),
315			Effort:       spec.Effort,
316			Once:         spec.Once,
317			PollInterval: spec.PollInterval.String(),
318			StdoutPath:   relToProject(m.projectRoot, logPath),
319			SpecPath:     relToProject(m.projectRoot, filepath.Join(dir, "spec.json")),
320			StartedAt:    time.Now().UTC(),
321		}
322		_ = m.writeRecord(dir, record)
323	
324		ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
325		logBuf := &bytes.Buffer{}
326		multiLog := io.MultiWriter(logBuf, logFile)
327	
328		progressCh := make(chan agent.ProgressEvent, 64)
329		handle := &workerHandle{
330			record:       record,
331			cancel:       cancel,
332			logBuf:       logBuf,
333			logFile:      logFile,
334			progressCh:   progressCh,
335			progressDone: make(chan struct{}),
336			lastPhaseTS:  time.Now().UTC(),
337		}
338	
339		m.mu.Lock()
340		m.workers[id] = handle
341		m.mu.Unlock()
342	
343		m.ensureWatchdog()
344	
345		go m.drainProgress(id, handle, progressCh)
346		go m.runWorker(ctx, id, dir, spec, effectiveRoot, handle, multiLog, eventsFile, progressCh)
347	
348		return record, nil
349	}

351	func (m *WorkerManager) runWorker(ctx context.Context, id, dir string, spec ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec, projectRoot string, handle *workerHandle, log io.Writer, eventSink io.WriteCloser, progressCh chan agent.ProgressEvent) {
352		if eventSink != nil {
353			defer eventSink.Close() //nolint:errcheck
354		}
355		store := bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx"))
356	
357		var worker *agent.ExecuteBeadWorker
358		if m.BeadWorkerFactory != nil {
359			worker = m.BeadWorkerFactory(store)
360		} else {
361			// Build an executor that calls agent.ExecuteBead in-process, then
362			// submits the result to the project's land coordinator. The
363			// coordinator (a single goroutine per projectRoot) serializes all
364			// target-ref writes for this project — this is the server-side
365			// implementation of the human-PR landing model. See ddx-8746d8a6
366			// for the rationale. Prior to this rewrite, runWorker never called
367			// LandBeadResult at all, so commits produced by server-managed
368			// workers were silently lost (ddx-e14efc58).
369			coordinator := m.LandCoordinators.Get(projectRoot)
370	
371			// singleTierAttempt runs one execution at a specific harness/model.
372			singleTierAttempt := func(ctx context.Context, beadID string, tier escalation.ModelTier, resolvedHarness, resolvedProvider, resolvedModel string) (agent.ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
373				gitOps := &agent.RealGitOps{}
374				attemptProvider := spec.Provider
375				if resolvedProvider != "" {
376					attemptProvider = resolvedProvider
377				}
378				res, err := agent.ExecuteBead(ctx, projectRoot, beadID, agent.ExecuteBeadOptions{
379					Harness:    resolvedHarness,
380					Model:      resolvedModel,
381					Provider:   attemptProvider,
382					ModelRef:   spec.ModelRef,
383					Effort:     spec.Effort,
384					BeadEvents: bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx")),
385				}, gitOps)
386				if err != nil && res == nil {
387					return agent.ExecuteBeadReport{}, err
388				}
389				if res != nil && res.ResultRev != "" && res.ResultRev != res.BaseRev && res.ExitCode == 0 {
390					if landErr := evaluateGatesAndSubmit(projectRoot, res, gitOps, coordinator, log); landErr != nil && err == nil {
391						err = landErr
392					}
393				} else if res != nil && res.ResultRev == res.BaseRev {
394					res.Outcome = "no-changes"
395					res.Status = agent.ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus(res.Outcome, res.ExitCode, res.Reason)
396				} else if res != nil && res.ExitCode != 0 {
397					res.Outcome = "preserved"
398					res.Status = agent.ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus(res.Outcome, res.ExitCode, res.Reason)
399				}
400				// Safety net: commit any leftover evidence files that Land()
401				// did not pick up (e.g. HEAD was detached, or no land ran).
402				if res != nil && res.AttemptID != "" {
403					_ = agent.VerifyCleanWorktree(projectRoot, res.AttemptID)
404				}
405				if err != nil {
406					return agent.ExecuteBeadReport{}, err
407				}
408				tierStr := ""
409				if tier != "" {
410					tierStr = string(tier)
411				}
412				return agent.ExecuteBeadReport{
413					BeadID:             res.BeadID,
414					AttemptID:          res.AttemptID,
415					WorkerID:           res.WorkerID,
416					Harness:            res.Harness,
417					Provider:           res.Provider,
418					Model:              res.Model,
419					Tier:               tierStr,
420					Status:             res.Status,
421					Detail:             res.Detail,
422					SessionID:          res.SessionID,
423					BaseRev:            res.BaseRev,
424					ResultRev:          res.ResultRev,
425					PreserveRef:        res.PreserveRef,
426					NoChangesRationale: res.NoChangesRationale,
427					CostUSD:            res.CostUSD,
428					DurationMS:         int64(res.DurationMS),
429				}, nil
430			}
431	
432			profile := agent.NormalizeRoutingProfile(spec.Profile)
433			cfg, _ := config.LoadWithWorkingDir(projectRoot)
434			var routingCfg *config.RoutingConfig
435			if cfg != nil && cfg.Agent != nil {
436				routingCfg = cfg.Agent.Routing
437			}
438	
439			// escalationEnabled when neither Harness nor Model is pinned.
440			escalationEnabled := spec.Harness == "" && spec.Model == ""
441	
442			// Cost-cap state shared by both single-attempt and tier-escalation
443			// paths within this worker run. Subscription / local providers do
444			// not contribute (see escalation.CountsTowardCostCap).
445			// TODO(ddx-785d02f7): expose maxCostUSD as a worker spec field
446			// once the spec config knob lands.
447			costCap := escalation.NewCostCapTracker(escalation.DefaultMaxCostUSD, func(harnessName string) bool {
448				svc, svcErr := agent.NewServiceFromWorkDir(projectRoot)
449				if svcErr != nil {
450					return true
451				}
452				infos, err := svc.ListHarnesses(context.Background())
453				if err != nil {
454					return true
455				}
456				for _, h := range infos {
457					if h.Name == harnessName {
458						return escalation.CountsTowardCostCap(h.IsLocal, h.IsSubscription, h.CostClass)
459					}
460				}
461				return true
462			})
463			accumulateBilledCost := func(report agent.ExecuteBeadReport) {
464				costCap.Add(report.Harness, report.CostUSD)
465			}
466			costCapTripped := func() (agent.ExecuteBeadReport, bool) {
467				detail, tripped := costCap.Tripped()
468				if !tripped {
469					return agent.ExecuteBeadReport{}, false
470				}
471				return agent.ExecuteBeadReport{
472					Status: agent.ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
473					Detail: detail,
474				}, true
475			}
476	
477			executor := agent.ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (agent.ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
478				if cappedReport, capped := costCapTripped(); capped {
479					cappedReport.BeadID = beadID
480					return cappedReport, nil
481				}
482				if !escalationEnabled {
483					report, err := singleTierAttempt(ctx, beadID, "", spec.Harness, spec.Provider, spec.Model)
484					if err == nil {
485						accumulateBilledCost(report)
486						if cappedReport, capped := costCapTripped(); capped {
487							cappedReport.BeadID = beadID
488							return cappedReport, nil
489						}
490					}
491					return report, err
492				}
493	
494				// Profile escalation: configured profile ladder bounded by MinTier/MaxTier.
495				tiers := agent.ResolveProfileLadder(routingCfg, profile, spec.MinTier, spec.MaxTier)
496				if len(tiers) == 0 {
497					return agent.ExecuteBeadReport{
498						BeadID: beadID,
499						Status: agent.ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
500						Detail: "execute-loop: no tiers in range (check min_tier / max_tier)",
501					}, nil
502				}
503	
504				beadStore := bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx"))
505				svc, svcErr := agent.NewServiceFromWorkDir(projectRoot)
506				if svcErr != nil {
507					return agent.ExecuteBeadReport{
508						BeadID: beadID,
509						Status: agent.ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
510						Detail: "execute-loop: failed to initialize routing service: " + svcErr.Error(),
511					}, nil
512				}
513				var lastReport agent.ExecuteBeadReport
514				var escalationAttempts []escalation.TierAttemptRecord
515				requestedTier := string(tiers[0])
516	
517				for tierIdx, tier := range tiers {
518					modelRefForTier := agent.ResolveTierModelRef(routingCfg, tier)
519					// Resolve the best harness for this tier via service.ResolveRoute.
520					dec, routeErr := svc.ResolveRoute(ctx, agentlib.RouteRequest{
521						Profile:   profile,
522						Model:     modelRefForTier,
523						Provider:  spec.Provider,
524						Reasoning: agentlib.Reasoning(spec.Effort),
525					})
526					probeResult := "ok"
527					// Treat cooldown-marked harnesses as unavailable for this tier.
528					// Health is owned by the upstream service; consult svc.RouteStatus.
529					if routeErr == nil && !workerHarnessHealthy(ctx, svc, dec.Harness) {
530						routeErr = fmt.Errorf("provider cooldown")
531					}
532					if routeErr != nil {
533						probeResult = "no viable provider"

685			NoReview:     spec.NoReview,

688		})

690		close(progressCh)
691		// Wait for drainProgress to process all remaining events (including live
692		// counter increments) before we overwrite handle.record with the final state.
693		<-handle.progressDone

695		m.mu.Lock()
696		record := handle.record
697		// Preserve terminal state set by Stop() or the watchdog so the final
698		// writeRecord below does not overwrite "stopped" / "reaped" with
699		// "exited" / "failed".
700		preservedState := ""
701		if record.State == "stopped" || record.State == "reaped" {
702			preservedState = record.State
703		}
704		record.FinishedAt = time.Now().UTC()

707		if err != nil {

709			record.Status = "failed"
710			record.Error = err.Error()

712		} else {
713			record.State = "exited"
714			record.Attempts = loopResult.Attempts

717	
718			if loopResult.NoReadyWork {

724				}
725			} else if loopResult.Successes > 0 {

727			} else {
728				record.Status = "exited"
729			}

731			if len(loopResult.Results) > 0 {
732				last := loopResult.Results[len(loopResult.Results)-1]
733				r := WorkerExecutionResult{
734					BeadID:     last.BeadID,
735					AttemptID:  last.AttemptID,
736					WorkerID:   last.WorkerID,
737					Harness:    last.Harness,

743					BaseRev:    last.BaseRev,
744					ResultRev:  last.ResultRev,
745					RetryAfter: last.RetryAfter,
746				}

748				record.LastResult = &r
749				if last.Detail != "" {
750					record.LastError = last.Detail
751				}
752				if last.Harness != "" && record.Harness == "" {
753					record.Harness = last.Harness
754				}
755				if last.Model != "" && record.Model == "" {
756					record.Model = last.Model
757				}
758				if last.Provider != "" && record.Provider == "" {
759					record.Provider = last.Provider
760				}
761			}
762		}
763		// Terminal-state override: if Stop() or the watchdog already marked
764		// this worker, keep that label so external consumers see the reason.
765		if preservedState != "" {
766			record.State = preservedState
767			record.Status = preservedState
768		}
769		_ = m.writeRecord(dir, record)
770		handle.record = record
771		m.mu.Unlock()
772	}

774	func (m *WorkerManager) List() ([]WorkerRecord, error) {
775		if err := os.MkdirAll(m.rootDir, 0o755); err != nil {
776			return nil, err
777		}
778		entries, err := os.ReadDir(m.rootDir)
779		if err != nil {
780			return nil, err
781		}
782		var out []WorkerRecord
783		for _, entry := range entries {
784			if !entry.IsDir() {
785				continue
786			}
787			rec, err := m.readRecord(filepath.Join(m.rootDir, entry.Name()))
788			if err != nil {
789				continue
790			}
791			out = append(out, rec)
792		}
793	
794		// Merge in-memory state for active workers
795		m.mu.Lock()
796		for i := range out {
797			if handle, ok := m.workers[out[i].ID]; ok {
798				out[i] = handle.record
799			}
800		}
801		m.mu.Unlock()
802	
803		sort.Slice(out, func(i, j int) bool {
804			return out[i].StartedAt.After(out[j].StartedAt)
805		})
806		return out, nil
807	}

820	// Stop performs a graceful termination of the worker:
821	//  1. Mark state=stopping and persist so observers see the transition.
822	//  2. Emit bead.stopped event + release the bead claim (if one is held).
823	//  3. Send SIGTERM to the worker's process group; escalate to SIGKILL
824	//     after WatchdogKillGrace if the leader is still alive. Pure-goroutine
825	//     workers have no PID — ctx cancellation below is the only lever.
826	//  4. Cancel the worker's context so the loop and in-flight executor exit.
827	//  5. Mark state=stopped and persist. runWorker preserves this terminal
828	//     state when it writes its final record.
829	//
830	// Stop is idempotent: a second call is a no-op. It returns an error only
831	// when the worker is unknown to the manager (already exited / never existed).
832	func (m *WorkerManager) Stop(id string) error {
833		m.mu.Lock()
834		handle := m.workers[id]
835		if handle == nil || handle.cancel == nil {
836			m.mu.Unlock()
837			return fmt.Errorf("worker not running")
838		}
839		if handle.stopped {
840			m.mu.Unlock()
841			return nil
842		}
843		handle.stopped = true
844	
845		now := time.Now().UTC()
846		projectRoot := handle.record.ProjectRoot
847		if projectRoot == "" {
848			projectRoot = m.projectRoot
849		}
850		pid := handle.record.PID
851		beadID := ""
852		if handle.record.CurrentAttempt != nil {
853			beadID = handle.record.CurrentAttempt.BeadID
854		}
855		if beadID == "" {
856			beadID = handle.record.CurrentBead
857		}
858		startedAt := handle.record.StartedAt
859		handle.record.State = "stopping"
860		handle.record.Status = "stopping"
861		dir := filepath.Join(m.rootDir, id)
862		transitionSnapshot := handle.record
863		cancel := handle.cancel
864		m.mu.Unlock()
865	
866		_ = m.writeRecord(dir, transitionSnapshot)
867	
868		// Release the bead claim first — this is durable and must not be
869		// leaked even if the SIGKILL path blocks for the full grace window.
870		if beadID != "" {
871			store := bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx"))
872			runtime := time.Duration(0)
873			if !startedAt.IsZero() {
874				runtime = now.Sub(startedAt)
875			}
876			body := fmt.Sprintf(
877				"worker=%s runtime=%s pid=%d reason=stop",
878				id, runtime.Round(time.Second), pid,
879			)
880			_ = store.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
881				Kind:      "bead.stopped",
882				Summary:   "stop",
883				Body:      body,
884				Actor:     "ddx",
885				Source:    "server-workers",
886				CreatedAt: now,
887			})
888			_ = store.Unclaim(beadID)
889		}
890	
891		// Escalate to the process group if we know the PID.
892		_, _, _, grace := m.watchdogDeadlines()
893		if pid > 0 {
894			terminateProcessGroup(pid, grace)
895		}
896	
897		// Cancel the worker goroutine so any in-process code sees context.Canceled.
898		cancel()
899	
900		// Flip in-memory state to the terminal "stopped" label. For real
901		// workers, runWorker's final writeRecord (with preservedState) will
902		// persist this to disk — we deliberately do not writeRecord here a
903		// second time because runWorker may still be mid-finalization and a
904		// double-write races the test cleanup. Idle handles (no runWorker)
905		// have their state observable in-memory; callers that need disk
906		// persistence for those can call writeRecord directly.
907		m.mu.Lock()
908		handle.record.State = "stopped"
909		handle.record.Status = "stopped"
910		// Only stamp FinishedAt for handles with no attached runWorker
911		// goroutine (logFile is the tell — StartExecuteLoop always sets it).
912		// For real workers, runWorker sets FinishedAt after its own cleanup.
913		if handle.logFile == nil && handle.record.FinishedAt.IsZero() {
914			handle.record.FinishedAt = time.Now().UTC()
915			finalSnapshot := handle.record
916			m.mu.Unlock()
917			_ = m.writeRecord(dir, finalSnapshot)
918			return nil
919		}
920		m.mu.Unlock()
921		return nil
922	}

924	// ensureWatchdog starts the supervisor goroutine exactly once per manager.
925	// The goroutine runs until StopWatchdog() is called (or the process exits).
926	func (m *WorkerManager) ensureWatchdog() {
927		m.watchdogOnce.Do(func() {
928			go m.watchdogLoop()
929		})
930	}
931	
932	// StopWatchdog halts the supervisor goroutine. Idempotent; used by tests.
933	func (m *WorkerManager) StopWatchdog() {
934		defer func() { _ = recover() }() // tolerate double-close
935		close(m.watchdogStop)
936	}
937	
938	// watchdogLoop periodically inspects every registered workerHandle and reaps
939	// those that have outlived WatchdogDeadline with no phase transition in
940	// StallDeadline.
941	func (m *WorkerManager) watchdogLoop() {
942		_, _, check, _ := m.watchdogDeadlines()
943		ticker := time.NewTicker(check)
944		defer ticker.Stop()
945	
946		for {
947			select {
948			case <-m.watchdogStop:
949				return
950			case <-ticker.C:
951				m.watchdogSweep(time.Now().UTC())
952			}
953		}
954	}
955	
956	// watchdogSweep inspects every handle once. Split out from watchdogLoop so
957	// tests can drive the check deterministically without relying on tickers.
958	func (m *WorkerManager) watchdogSweep(now time.Time) {
959		watchdogDL, stallDL, _, _ := m.watchdogDeadlines()
960	
961		type candidate struct {
962			id      string
963			handle  *workerHandle
964			runtime time.Duration
965			stalled time.Duration
966			beadID  string
967			pid     int
968		}
969	
970		m.mu.Lock()
971		var picks []candidate
972		for id, h := range m.workers {
973			if h == nil || h.reaped {
974				continue
975			}
976			rec := h.record
977			if !rec.FinishedAt.IsZero() {
978				continue
979			}
980			if rec.StartedAt.IsZero() {
981				continue
982			}
983			runtime := now.Sub(rec.StartedAt)
984			if runtime <= watchdogDL {
985				continue
986			}
987			// Stall check — require an in-flight attempt; a worker that is
988			// between beads (CurrentAttempt == nil) has no phase to wedge on.
989			if rec.CurrentAttempt == nil {
990				continue
991			}
992			lastPhase := h.lastPhaseTS
993			if lastPhase.IsZero() {
994				lastPhase = rec.StartedAt
995			}
996			stalled := now.Sub(lastPhase)
997			if stalled <= stallDL {
998				continue
999			}
1000	
1001			beadID := ""
1002			if rec.CurrentAttempt != nil {
1003				beadID = rec.CurrentAttempt.BeadID
1004			}
1005			if beadID == "" {
1006				beadID = rec.CurrentBead
1007			}
1008	
1009			h.reaped = true
1010			picks = append(picks, candidate{
1011				id:      id,
1012				handle:  h,
1013				runtime: runtime,
1014				stalled: stalled,
1015				beadID:  beadID,
1016				pid:     rec.PID,
1017			})
1018		}
1019		m.mu.Unlock()
1020	
1021		for _, c := range picks {
1022			m.reapWorker(c.id, c.handle, c.pid, c.beadID, c.runtime, c.stalled, "watchdog")
1023		}
1024	}
1025	
1026	// reapWorker performs the escalation for a stalled worker:
1027	//  1. Emit bead.reaped event on the bead tracker (if a bead is claimed).
1028	//  2. Release the bead claim (Unclaim → status=open).
1029	//  3. SIGTERM → grace → SIGKILL the worker's process group, if a PID is
1030	//     registered. Fall back to ctx cancellation for pure-goroutine workers.
1031	//  4. Mark the WorkerRecord state=reaped and persist it.
1032	func (m *WorkerManager) reapWorker(id string, handle *workerHandle, pid int, beadID string, runtime, stalled time.Duration, reason string) {
1033		now := time.Now().UTC()
1034	
1035		m.mu.Lock()
1036		rec := handle.record
1037		projectRoot := rec.ProjectRoot
1038		if projectRoot == "" {
1039			projectRoot = m.projectRoot
1040		}
1041		m.mu.Unlock()
1042	
1043		// 1. Emit the reap event and release the bead claim before killing, so
1044		//    the claim is not leaked even if the kill blocks for the full grace.
1045		if beadID != "" {
1046			store := bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx"))
1047			body := fmt.Sprintf(
1048				"worker=%s runtime=%s stalled=%s pid=%d reason=%s",
1049				id, runtime.Round(time.Second), stalled.Round(time.Second), pid, reason,
1050			)
1051			_ = store.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
1052				Kind:      "bead.reaped",
1053				Summary:   reason,
1054				Body:      body,
1055				Actor:     "ddx-watchdog",
1056				Source:    "server-workers",
1057				CreatedAt: now,
1058			})
1059			_ = store.Unclaim(beadID)
1060		}
1061	
1062		// 2. Escalate to the worker process group if we know the PID.
1063		_, _, _, grace := m.watchdogDeadlines()
1064		if pid > 0 {
1065			terminateProcessGroup(pid, grace)
1066		}
1067	
1068		// 3. Cancel the goroutine so any in-process code sees context.Canceled.
1069		if handle.cancel != nil {
1070			handle.cancel()
1071		}
1072	
1073		// 4. Flip state=reaped and persist. runWorker may still race to
1074		//    overwrite this with "exited" when it returns; that's fine — the
1075		//    bead.reaped event plus released claim are the durable record.
1076		m.mu.Lock()
1077		handle.record.State = "reaped"
1078		handle.record.Status = "reaped"
1079		handle.record.ReapReason = reason
1080		if handle.record.FinishedAt.IsZero() {
1081			handle.record.FinishedAt = now
1082		}
1083		if handle.record.LastError == "" {
1084			handle.record.LastError = fmt.Sprintf("watchdog reaped worker after runtime=%s stalled=%s",
1085				runtime.Round(time.Second), stalled.Round(time.Second))
1086		}
1087		dir := filepath.Join(m.rootDir, id)
1088		snapshot := handle.record
1089		m.mu.Unlock()
1090	
1091		_ = m.writeRecord(dir, snapshot)
1092	}

1098			sessionLog := m.readActiveSessionLog(handle)
1099			m.mu.Unlock()
1100			if sessionLog != "" {
1101				return log + "\n" + sessionLog, "", nil
1102			}
1103			return log, "", nil
1104		}
1105		m.mu.Unlock()
1106	
1107		// Fall back to reading from disk for completed workers
1108		rec, err := m.Show(id)

1122	// drainProgress reads ProgressEvents from ch and:
1123	//  1. Updates the WorkerRecord's CurrentAttempt, RecentPhases, and LastAttempt fields.
1124	//  2. Broadcasts each event to all active SSE subscribers for the worker.
1125	//
1126	// It runs as a goroutine alongside runWorker; it exits when ch is closed.
1127	func (m *WorkerManager) drainProgress(workerID string, handle *workerHandle, ch <-chan agent.ProgressEvent) {
1128		const maxRecentPhases = 20
1129		for evt := range ch {
1130			m.mu.Lock()
1131			rec := handle.record
1132	
1133			if !evt.Heartbeat {
1134				// Phase-transition: record in RecentPhases (capped at maxRecentPhases)
1135				rec.RecentPhases = append(rec.RecentPhases, PhaseTransition{
1136					Phase:    evt.Phase,
1137					TS:       evt.TS,
1138					PhaseSeq: evt.PhaseSeq,
1139				})
1140				if len(rec.RecentPhases) > maxRecentPhases {

1233	func (m *WorkerManager) SubscribeProgress(workerID string) (<-chan agent.ProgressEvent, func()) {
1234		m.mu.Lock()
1235		defer m.mu.Unlock()
1236	
1237		handle, ok := m.workers[workerID]
1238		if !ok {
1239			// Worker never started or was never registered
1240			ch := make(chan agent.ProgressEvent)
1241			close(ch)
1242			return ch, func() {}
1243		}
1244	
1245		// Check if drainProgress has already exited (worker done)
1246		if handle.progressDone != nil {
1247			select {
1248			case <-handle.progressDone:
1249				ch := make(chan agent.ProgressEvent)
1250				close(ch)
1251				return ch, func() {}
1252			default:
1253			}
1254		}
1255	
1256		ch := make(chan agent.ProgressEvent, 64)
1257		handle.progressSubs = append(handle.progressSubs, ch)
1258	
1259		unsub := func() {
1260			m.mu.Lock()
1261			defer m.mu.Unlock()
1262			if h, ok := m.workers[workerID]; ok {
1263				for i, sub := range h.progressSubs {
1264					if sub == ch {
1265						h.progressSubs = append(h.progressSubs[:i], h.progressSubs[i+1:]...)
1266						break
1267					}
1268				}
1269			}
1270		}

1285		var newest string

1287		cutoff := time.Now().Add(-30 * time.Minute)
1288		for _, entry := range entries {

1290				continue
1291			}

1293			// entries. Loop milestone progress is already captured in worker.log.

1297			info, err := entry.Info()
1298			if err != nil {
1299				continue
1300			}
1301			if info.ModTime().After(newestMod) && info.ModTime().After(cutoff) {
1302				newest = filepath.Join(logDir, entry.Name())
1303				newestMod = info.ModTime()
1304			}
1305		}
1306		if newest == "" {
1307			return ""
1308		}
1309

1311		data, err := os.ReadFile(newest)
1312		if err != nil {
1313			return ""
1314		}

1316		lines := strings.Split(strings.TrimSpace(string(data)), "\n")
1317		// Show the last 50 entries
1318		start := 0

1383	// evaluateGatesAndSubmit runs the required-gate evaluation BEFORE submitting
1384	// res to the coordinator. When a required gate fails (or a ratchet misses),
1385	// it preserves the result directly via update-ref and skips coordinator
1386	// submission entirely — Land() stays a pure ref-advance contract; gate
1387	// enforcement happens upstream. When gates pass (or no governing IDs are
1388	// declared in the manifest), it submits the LandRequest to the coordinator
1389	// and applies the LandResult onto res.
1390	//
1391	// Mirrors the interactive path in cmd/agent_execute_bead.go. The preserve
1392	// reason/status fields are set the same way ApplyLandingToResult would set
1393	// them on the same scenario, so server and interactive paths produce
1394	// identical preserve evidence.
1395	//
1396	// Returns the coordinator submit error when one occurs; gate-context and
1397	// gate-eval errors are soft-logged and treated as no-eval (the existing
1398	// submit path continues).
1399	func evaluateGatesAndSubmit(
1400		projectRoot string,
1401		res *agent.ExecuteBeadResult,
1402		gitOps agent.GitOps,
1403		coordinator gateLandSubmitter,
1404		log io.Writer,
1405	) error {
1406		wt, ids, cleanup, ctxErr := agent.BuildLandingGateContext(projectRoot, res, gitOps)
1407		if ctxErr != nil {
1408			// Soft-fail: log and skip gate eval rather than abort the land.
1409			_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(log, "ddx: warning: gate-context setup failed: %v (skipping required-gate eval)\n", ctxErr)
1410		}
1411		defer cleanup()
1412	
1413		if wt != "" {
1414			checksAbs := filepath.Join(projectRoot, res.ExecutionDir, "checks.json")
1415			checksRel := filepath.Join(res.ExecutionDir, "checks.json")
1416			anyFailed, ratchetFailed, evalErr := agent.EvaluateRequiredGatesForResult(wt, ids, res, projectRoot, checksAbs, checksRel)
1417			if evalErr != nil {
1418				// Log and treat as no-eval; existing path continues.
1419				_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(log, "ddx: warning: gate evaluation failed: %v (skipping)\n", evalErr)
1420			} else if anyFailed || ratchetFailed {
1421				// Preserve directly. Mirror LandBeadResult's preserve path so the
1422				// server produces identical evidence to the interactive path.
1423				// PreserveRef helper produces refs/ddx/iterations/<bead>/<ts>-<shortSHA>;
1424				// using it keeps server- and interactive-managed evidence indistinguishable.
1425				preserveRef := agent.PreserveRef(res.BeadID, res.BaseRev)
1426				if upErr := gitOps.UpdateRef(projectRoot, preserveRef, res.ResultRev); upErr != nil {
1427					_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(log, "ddx: warning: preserving result ref %s failed: %v\n", preserveRef, upErr)
1428				} else {
1429					res.PreserveRef = preserveRef
1430				}
1431				res.Outcome = "preserved"
1432				if ratchetFailed {
1433					res.Reason = agent.RatchetPreserveReason
1434				} else {
1435					res.Reason = "post-run checks failed"
1436				}
1437				res.Status = agent.ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus(res.Outcome, res.ExitCode, res.Reason)
1438				res.Detail = agent.ExecuteBeadStatusDetail(res.Status, res.Reason, res.Error)
1439				return nil
1440			}
1441		}
1442	
1443		// Gates passed (or no governing IDs / soft-failure): submit to coordinator.
1444		landReq := agent.BuildLandRequestFromResult(projectRoot, res)
1445		landRes, landErr := coordinator.Submit(landReq)
1446		if landErr != nil {

1448		}
1449		agent.ApplyLandResultToExecuteBeadResult(res, landRes)

1451	}

135	func (s *Server) ListenAndServe() error {

157	func (s *Server) ListenAndServeTLS(certFile, keyFile string) error {
158		if certFile == "" || keyFile == "" {
159			tlsDir := filepath.Join(s.WorkingDir, ".ddx", "server", "tls")
160			var err error
161			certFile, keyFile, err = ensureSelfSignedCert(tlsDir)
162			if err != nil {
163				return fmt.Errorf("generating self-signed cert: %w", err)
164			}
165		}
166		s.writeAddrFile("https")
167		if s.TsnetConfig != nil && s.TsnetConfig.Enabled {
168			errCh := make(chan error, 2)
169			go func() {
170				errCh <- http.ListenAndServeTLS(s.Addr, certFile, keyFile, s.mux)
171			}()
172			go func() {
173				errCh <- s.listenTsnet()
174			}()
175			return <-errCh
176		}
177		return http.ListenAndServeTLS(s.Addr, certFile, keyFile, s.mux)
178	}

248	func (s *ServerState) RegisterProject(path string) ProjectEntry {
249		s.mu.Lock()

251	
252		canonical := resolvedProjectPath(path)
253		if canonical == "" {
254			canonical = path
255		}
256	
257		now := time.Now().UTC()
258		id := projectID(canonical)
259		name := filepath.Base(canonical)
260		remote := resolveGitRemote(canonical)
261	
262		for i, p := range s.Projects {
263			if p.ID == id {
264				s.Projects[i].LastSeen = now
265				s.Projects[i].Path = canonical // normalise in case of symlink change
266				s.Projects[i].Name = name
267				// Clear any stale tombstone on re-registration.
268				s.Projects[i].Unreachable = false
269				s.Projects[i].TombstonedAt = nil
270				if remote != "" {
271					s.Projects[i].GitRemote = remote
272				}
273				return s.Projects[i]
274			}
275		}
276	
277		entry := ProjectEntry{
278			ID:           id,
279			Name:         name,
280			Path:         canonical,
281			GitRemote:    remote,
282			RegisteredAt: now,
283			LastSeen:     now,
284		}
285		s.Projects = append(s.Projects, entry)
286		return entry
287	}

git -c commit.gpgsign=false commit -m "fix(server): workersByProject filters by project path, not id [ddx-05b4cc9d]

GetWorkersGraphQL(projectID) compared the GraphQL project id argument
directly to WorkerRecord.ProjectRoot (a filesystem path), so every worker
was filtered out whenever a non-empty id was passed. The per-project
Workers view on the project home page was therefore always empty even
when a Drain-queue dispatch had started a worker.

Resolve the id to its canonical project path via ServerState.GetProjectByID
before filtering, matching the approach already used by GetBeadSnapshots.
An unknown project id returns an empty list (not an error), consistent
with the prior behavior for non-matching filters.

Chose Option 1 (id→path resolution at the filter site) over Option 2
(store project id on WorkerRecord): one-line change, no schema or
migration, no coupling between WorkerManager and ServerState required.

Tests:
- TestGraphQLWorkersByProjectScopedByID (new): two registered projects
  with distinct paths/ids, one worker each; asserts workersByProject
  returns only the matching worker per project id, unknown id returns
  empty, and the global workers query still returns both.
- TestGraphQLWorkers (updated): now queries by the registered project
  id instead of the working-directory path, so the assertion actually
  exercises the resolver rather than relying on the bug.

Unblocks the Workers-lifecycle-controls bead (ddx-69789664) whose
acceptance assumed the Workers pane worked correctly."
